analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet emphasizes China’s long history, long-term thinking, and resolve to achieve the “two centenary goals,” asserting this is a concrete national plan and asking others to trust China. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (West immoral/hostile): Not mentioned; no criticism of the West. CH2 (China as benevolent power): The tweet does not discuss international cooperation, law, or global benefits; it is domestically focused. CH3 (China has an epic history): Yes—explicit reference to China’s long history and future-oriented mindset, implying historical continuity and resilience. CH4 (appropriate political system and honorable values): Implicitly, the claim of a real national plan and call to “please believe us” suggests confidence in China’s governance and values aligned with people’s will; however, it lacks explicit mention of political system or values like socialism/democracy. Still, the emphasis on national planning and legitimacy can fit CH4. CH5 (CCP government succeeds): The tweet asserts determination to achieve the two centenary goals (a signature CCP agenda), portraying effective national planning and future success; this aligns with CH5. CH6 (culture/nature/heritage): Not applicable; no cultural or touristic content. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH3 and CH5 clearly; CH4 is reasonably supported by the framing of national plan and trust in governance.
